observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to review the existing practices in conducting physical count of its PPE and maintaining property and accounting records to address the noted deficiencies.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
Direct the IC to conduct a proper and complete physical count of PPE and ensure that all PPE items are physically counted;
Direct the MA and the MGSO to maintain complete and proper accounting and property records relative to PPE items; and
Require the concerned officers and employees responsible for completing the donation process for the remaining PPE items scheduled for donation.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to:
Direct the MGSO to conduct physical count of all inventory items every semester and prepare the RPCI within the period prescribed by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs. Thereafter, require the MGSO and the MA to reconcile the RPCI and the accounting records for any variance;
Consider allocating funds for the construction of storage facility for inventory items and require the MGSO to maintain the required SCs for each type of inventories, in compliance with the pertinent guidelines; and
Require the MA to review meticulously the SLCs prepared and maintained by the accounting office, ensuring that they contain all the necessary details required by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to:
Verify the validity of AP balances aged two years or more. If these obligations are found to be invalid or unclaimed, initiate the reversion of the outstanding AP amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the GF; and
Notify the concerned suppliers and contractors in writing regarding their unclaimed accounts. Advise them that if no proper claim is made within a reasonable period, the amount will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the GF of the MGJ.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the:
MA and the MT to prioritize the reconciliation of the remaining discrepancies, with particular focus on the remaining unrecorded book reconciling items in the GLs/SLs and proper maintenance of cashbooks to ensure proper cash monitoring and accurate presentation of balances in the FS; and
MA to conduct a comprehensive investigation of the subsidiary records to locate details of the unaccounted debit and credit memos and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the reconciling items. This process may involve sending a communication letter to the Authorized Government Depository Bank for confirmation of the details.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MT and MA to continue the periodic and regular reconciliation of the Cash-Local Treasury account and equally prioritize the investigation of unreconciled prior period balances to ensure accurate reporting. Moreover, to immediately correct/adjust their respective records upon identification of errors during the reconciliation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Ensure strict adherence with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other the relevant provisions in the preparation of LDRRMFIP. Moreover, to ensure that the planned PPAs for the year are detailed in the LDRRMFIP; and
Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the basic requirements for the extension of contract time are strictly complied with in accordance with Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and that proper documentation is observed at all times to warrant the propriety of the transactions of the Municipality.
We also recommended and Management agreed to apply the calibrated actions as provided in GPPB Circular 03-2019, if necessary, to prevent delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Maintain adequate monitoring of project implementation in order to ensure that projects are completed within schedule and according to specifications; and
Coordinate closely with the concerned contractors to ensure that issues are addressed and that milestones are met. Also, to enforce stricter demands, preferably in writing, whenever the contractors incur substantial negative slippage that may affect the period of completion of projects.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to:
Require the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO), MA and MT to conduct a thorough investigation of the identified disparity. This collaborative effort should involve coordinating with the BIR to ensure a seamless reconciliation process; and
Instruct the MA to prepare necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of the Due to BIR account.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the HRMO, MA, and MT to collaboratively conduct a thorough examination of the Due to PhilHealth account balances, focusing on the reasons behind prolonged outstanding amounts over multiple years;
Instruct the MA to request confirmation of these balances from the relevant Government Owned and Controlled Corporation. Subsequently, if required, prepare any necessary adjusting entries; and
Maintain and enhance the MGJ’s commendable efforts in accurately deducting and promptly remitting PhilHealth contributions in strict adherence to the established laws, rules, and regulations of the National Health Insurance.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the HRMO, MA and the MT to conduct a thorough reconciliation between the amounts withheld or deducted and the amounts remitted, aiming to ascertain the accurate sums owed to GSIS/HDMF;
Instruct the MA to request confirmation of these balances from the relevant GOCCs. Subsequently, if required, prepare any necessary adjusting entries; and
Instruct the HRMO with enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of payroll preparation processes to ensure comprehensive inclusion of all employees in the remittance list as soon as their contributions and amortizations are deducted from their gross earnings.

observations.reason: Lack of management initiative.
Ongoing implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the MGSO to conduct a physical count of all inventory items semestrally and render a report thereon within the prescribed period;

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12, Inventories, and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and

Require the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain and update the stock cards and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd Public Schools District Supervisor (PSDS) and Schools Division Superintendent  (SDS) as well as the requiredSchool Improvement Plan(SIPs)andDivision Education Development Plan (DEDPs) and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB must strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.
observations.managementAction: Management/LSB to closely coordinate with the two School Districts of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Numerous changes in focal person during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts as required under Item No. 7 of COA Circular No. 2016-005. For those unsettled receivables supported with legal and valid documents, send demand letters to concerned agencies/association/personnel for them to settle immediately, while those account where settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, we recommended that Management follow the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Management will request authority to write-off dormant receivables after exhausting all efforts of collection.
observations.reason: Absence of supporting documents from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management dispose the unserviceable properties based on the most appropriate mode of disposal pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Sale of unserviceable properties will be conducted after appraisal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the MA to disclose information on the LRN in the Notes to FS;
Provide resources/manpower to the Municipal Engineer in assisting the MA and MGSO in identifying the complete description and cost segregation of road components; and
Require the MGSO to maintain RLRN, RPCRNS, LRN Property Card and Local road map in standard format.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the MGSO to conduct physical count of all inventory items semestrally and render a report thereon within the prescribed period; and
Require the MGSO and the MA to maintain properly and update the SCs and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
the MA to coordinate with the MGSO and Municipal Assessor’s Office to:
derecognize in the books the one parcel of land granted by the previous years’ administration to a recipient as donation; and
verify the validity of ownership of three parcels of land with total book value of ₱1.35 million already recorded in the books but not supported by TCTs nor TDs;
the Municipal Assessor to continue acting with dispatch on their plan to conduct ocular inspection of lands with TDs under the name of the Municipality of Juban, and if warranted, have these titled in favor of the Municipality; and
the MGSO, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office to sustain their vigorous efforts in working on the reconstitution of titles for the parcels of land owned by the Municipality in which copies of titles could no longer be provided by the Registry of Deeds.

We recommended and LCE agreed to require the MA the following courses of actions:
To request again or follow-up from the concerned depository banks copies of the DMs/CMs to determine their validity and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the affected account balances; and
To coordinate closely with the Treasury Office to exert extra effort to retrieve available documents; and
Ensure that all book reconciling items for a given period are recorded in the books of accounts. In addition, bank confirmation be made regularly to validate actual existence of accounts and bank balances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer to observe strictly the rules and regulations provided by DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016 specifically for the inclusion of items for project billboard and safe and heath in the preparation of the ABC.
We recommended and the LCE direct the MDRRMO to include all sources of LDRRMF in the preparation of LDRRMFIP in succeeding years as mandated by COA Circular NO. 2012-002.

We recommended and the LSB agreed to:
Approved strictly the SEF Budget not later than October 31 of the current year;
Include the savings from immediately preceding year as beginning balance in the preparation of annual budget and/or enact a supplemental budget to finance the priority projects as embodied in Section 272 of RA 7160; and
Utilize efficiently and effectively the SEF Budget by implementing fully all identified PPAs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to:
Reconcile the amounts withheld or deducted with the amounts remitted to determine the actual amounts of Due to BIR. Thereafter, prepare and record the necessary adjusting entry, if any; and
Coordinate with the previous MA to retrieve the details of the prior year transactions and prepare subsidiary ledgers (SLs).

We commended Management of its compliance with the rules and regulations prescribed by the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth for CY 2021 transactions.  As to un-reconciled balances from previous years and minor deficiencies in current year transactions, we recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Instruct the MA to exert effort in retrieving the details of previous years’ un-reconciled balances and request confirmation of such balances with the concerned GOCCs. Thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entry, if any;
Direct the Human Resource Department Head to improve the efficiency and data capture in payroll preparation so that all employees are captured in the remittance list once their contributions and amortizations are deducted from their gross earnings; and
Consider initiating the development or acquisition of a computerized payroll system for a more accurate and efficient processing of the employee’s payroll and related accounts.

observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation of records between the MGSO and accounting was conducted for the current year only, due to absences of prior years’ documents and reports.

No Action taken by Management.

Not able to conduct physical count inventory and inspection of all unserviceable PPE due to limited manpower and time constraints.

No Action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: Regular reconciliation of cashbooks and accounting records was conducted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

File a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA as prescribed by the aforecited Circular; and 


Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of receivable accounts to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant will file a request for authority to write-off in CY 2022.

Allowance for Impairment was provided in CY 2021.
observations.reason: No request for write-off submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management utilize the QRF only after the issuance of a Local Sanggunian Resolution to support such release including an itemized utilization thereof pursuant to Item No. 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We likewise request Management to justify why the disbursements of lump-sum appropriation without prior authorization from the Local Sanggunian and indiscriminate charging of expenses without anitemized or specific PPAs in the budget may not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Justification on disbursement of lump-sum appropriation was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of payable accounts to ensure that only valid payables are recorded in the books; and

Prepare a certified schedule of ageing of dormant payable accounts for which there are no valid claimants/creditors and no supporting documents, thereafter, revert the verified amounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Government Equity Account by preparing the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Trace and locate the relevant documents to serve as basis in analyzing the reconciling items and prepare adjustments, if any; and

Conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the discrepancy.

We recommended that Management continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

observations.managementAction: Appropriations for titling of the land owned by the Municipality, however, time constraints in collating supporting documents hindered the completion of titling of lands.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the records of prior years’ transactions and prepare adjusting journal entries to present the correct Due to BIR account balance.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and analysis of the inter-agency payable accounts, including confirmation with the respective government agencies, to determine the correct balance of the said accounts and thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books.

We likewise recommended the immediate remittance of amounts withheld to the concerned government agencies and ensure that monthly remittances correspond to the exact amounts withheld to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and so that the Municipal officials and employees could enjoy the benefits due them.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue and strengthen coordination with the Municipal Engineer to determine the condition of the infrastructure assets constructed in CYs 2015 and below, in order to assess the carrying amounts of the said assets and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Infrastructure Asset accounts balance in the FS;

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation ofReport on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN); and

Direct the Inventory Committee and MGSO to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and Report of Local Road Network (RLRN), respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLCs) and Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs), respectively, for local road projects to ensure proper recording and control of the assets.
observations.reason: Limited manpower and absence of prior years’ supporting documents prevented the updating/reconciliation.  Nevertheless, PPELCs are maintained in the current year.

observations.managementAction:
Notices of delinquencies were sent out to RPT declared owners and posted in conspicuous public places (e. g. SB Bulletin Board, Municipal Hall Entrance). However, collections were still affected by the ongoing pandemic.

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office will closely coordinate with Assessor, Provincial Treasurer’s Office and Accounting for the ‘clean up’ of RPT/SET receivable accounts

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Secure copies of debit memos and credit memos from the concerned banks to support the adjustments to be made in the books and make proper representation with the concerned bank to correct the bank error; and

Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books for all valid book reconciling items in order to arrive at a correct Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: Management will make follow ups and/or refurnish written request to concerned banks to determine nature of CMs and DMs for proper take up in the books.
observations.reason: Absence of supporting documents from previous years to properly record most of the bank CMs and DMs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

Municipal Treasurer to update the certified list of real property and special education taxes due and collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card and submit this to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET receivables; and

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile the balance of the /SET receivables account with the list of taxpayers with paid/unpaid real property tax dues maintained by the Municipal Treasurer to come up with an accurate /SET receivables.
observations.managementAction:
Manual records are maintained by the MTO and will take a lot of time to update the list. Also, the limited manpower hindered the updating.

SLs for RPT and SET were not yet maintained by OMA. | In the beginning of year 2022 and to address the need of the LGU, Management has employed one JO with a degree and work experience in Information Technology.

The MTO will coordinate with other LGUs that have employed IT software/program/system in managing taxpayers’ records and propose to the Management procurement of similar IT software/program/system that bests meet LGU-Juban’s needs.

The MTO will closely coordinate with Assessor, PTO and Accounting for the ‘clean up’ of RPT/SET receivable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management update the 2009 Revenue and Tax Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable projects of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The Legislative Branch of the Agency opted to defer discussion during this time of pandemic:

In consideration of easing the financial burden to the public since revision entails imposing higher taxes


Restrictions on face-to-face meeting for discussion and information dissemination to the public.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned personnel to secure copies of the Torrens Title or any equivalent document of all land properties registered under the name of the Municipality from the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and ensure proper safekeeping of the newly secured and subsequently acquired Land Titles.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of securing land titles.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue and strengthen coordination with the Municipal Engineer to determine the condition of the infrastructure assets constructed in CYs 2015 and below, in order to assess the carrying amounts of the said assets and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Infrastructure Asset accounts balance in the FS;

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN); and

Direct the Inventory Committee and MGSO to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and Report of Local Road Network (RLRN), respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/ Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLCs) and Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs), respectively, for local road projects to ensure proper recording and control of the assets.
observations.reason: Limited manpower and absence of prior years’ supporting documents prevented the updating/reconciliation.  Nevertheless, PPELCs are maintained in the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The MGSO initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the Municipality at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and

The Municipal Accountant and MGSO maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled.
observations.reason: Absence of previous years supporting documents and lack of personnel and stock room hindered implementation of Perpetual Inventory System.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd Public Schools District Supervisor (PSDS) and  Schools Division Superintendent  (SDS) as well as the required School Improvement Plan (SIPs) and Division Education Development Plan  (DEDPs) and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB must strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.

Likewise, we recommended that the LSB strictly comply with the requirements of the said JC in identifying the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenses and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which it was established.

Finally, justification is requested why the non-eligible expenses may not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: Numerous changes in focal person during the year.

For those unsettled receivables supported with legal and valid documents, send demand letters to concerned agencies/association/personnel for them to settle immediately, while those account where settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, we recommended that Management follow the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the MGSO to conduct a physical count of all inventory items semestrally and render a report thereon within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12, Inventories, and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and
Require the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain and update the stock cards and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.

We recommended that Management:
Reconcile thoroughly the Cash in Bank accounts in order to come up with the correct balances of the said accounts presented in the FS;
Ensure that all valid reconciling items to be recorded and adjusted in the books are properly supported with complete documents; and
Exercise due diligence and professional care in the conduct of reconciliation of records and in the performance of functions especially in the recording of journal entries and mathematical computation thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
File a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA as prescribed by the aforecited Circular; and
Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of receivable accounts to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books.

We recommended that Management utilize the QRF only after the issuance of a Local Sanggunian Resolution to support such release including an itemized utilization thereof pursuant to Item No. 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of payable accounts to ensure that only valid payables are recorded in the books; and
Prepare a certified schedule of ageing of dormant payable accounts for which there are no valid claimants/creditors and no supporting documents, thereafter, revert the verified amounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Government Equity Account by preparing the necessary JEVs as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Trace and locate the relevant documents to serve as basis in analyzing the reconciling items and prepare adjustments, if any; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the discrepancy.

We recommended that Management continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

On the other hand, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the records of prior years’ transactions and prepare adjusting journal entries to present the correct Due to BIR account balance.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and analysis of the inter-agency payable accounts, including confirmation with the respective government agencies, to determine the correct balance of the said accounts and thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books.
We likewise recommended the immediate remittance of amounts withheld to the concerned government agencies and ensure that monthly remittances correspond to the exact amounts withheld to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and so that the Municipal officials and employees could enjoy the benefits due them.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that Management:

Require the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO) and the Municipal Accounting Office to prioritize the reconciliation process and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and adjusted in the books accordingly;

Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) to support GL account balances and the MGSO to keep corresponding PCs for control purposes; and

Require the MGSO to conduct physical inventory and inspection of all unserviceable and obsolete items so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.
observations.reason: Current year balances are tally; while, absence of supporting documents from previous years precluded reconciliation of prior years’ discrepancies.

Some PPAs were not implemented due to the ongoing construction of the new municipal hall, no turnover yet of evacuation center and gymnasium, and restrictions on gatherings and travel imposed by the Inter-Agency Task Force on Emerging Infectious Diseases (IATF-EID) during the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strategize how to efficiently and effectively collect taxes and consider, among others, the following measures:

Conduct information and education campaign/activities, e.g. social media/radio, and other means of communication to encourage land owners to pay regularly their tax obligation, impart the benefits of paying early and/or on time and consider condonation of fines and penalties to delinquent tax payers;

Develop collection strategies to enhance collection of taxes like seeking the assistance of barangay officials in the house-to house campaign; and

Post the notice of delinquency in the payment of RPT in the main entrance of the municipal building and other conspicuous public places frequently visited by people.
observations.reason: Notice of delinquencies were sent out to RPT declared owners and posted in conspicuous public places (e. g. SB Bulletin Board, Municipal Hall Entrance). However, collections were still affected by the ongoing pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant consider the proposed accounting entries on advance payment of RPT and SET in order to reflect the correct amount of RPT and SET Income, Due to LGUs and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management strictly maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We also recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is used solely for development projects.
observations.reason:
Funds were not fully utilized during the year since the Agency has considered:

Deficiencies noted in some projects awaiting compliance from contractors, and

Agency preparedness due to a possible implementation of a lockdown by 4th quarter.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management:

Expedite the submission of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) to serve as guide for the efficient management of solid waste generated within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and 

Prioritize SWM programs and projects by providing/allocating enough budget for their full implementation; 

We also recommended that Management encourage the remaining five barangays to establish their own Material Recovery Facility (MRFs) for the collection, segregation, recycling of biodegradable, recyclable, compostable and reusable wastes.

We recommended that Management maximize the implementation of the identified and prioritized programs to ensure efficient utilization of the fund so that its objectives are fully achieved in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017.

We recommended that Management require the:

Municipal Treasurer to update the certified list of real property and special education taxes due and collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card and submit this to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET receivables; and

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile the balance of the /SET receivables account with the list of taxpayers with paid/unpaid real property tax dues maintained by the Municipal Treasurer to come up with an accurate /SET receivables.
observations.reason:
Manual records are maintained by the MTO and will take a lot of time to update the list. Also, the limited manpower hindered the updating.

SLs for RPT and SET were not yet maintained by OMA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management update the 2009 Revenue and Tax Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable projects of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The Legislative Branch of the Agency opted to defer discussion during this time of pandemic:

In consideration of easing the financial burden to the public since revision entails imposing higher taxes

Restrictions on face-to-face meeting for discussion and information dissemination to the public.
The Municipality is still in the process of securing titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts as required under Item No. 7 of COA Circular No. 2016-005. For those unsettled receivables supported with legal and valid documents, send demand letters to concerned agencies/association/personnel for them to settle immediately, while those account where settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, we recommended that Management follow the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: There was a change of Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Secure copies of debit memos and credit memos from the concerned banks to support the adjustments to be made in the books and make proper representation with the concerned bank to correct the bank error;
Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books for all valid book reconciling items in order to arrive at a correct Cash in Bank account; and
Ensure that all subsequent valid reconciling items to be recorded and adjusted in the books are properly supported with complete documents.
observations.reason:
Untimely resignation of Municipal Accountant and the newly hired accountant is still coping with the workload.

No adjustments were made due to change of accountant.

observations.managementAction: Sale of unserviceable properties will be conducted after appraisal.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue and strengthen coordination with the Municipal Engineer to determine the condition of the infrastructure assets constructed in CYs 2015 and below, in order to assess the carrying amounts of the said assets and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Infrastructure Asset accounts balance in the FS;
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN; and
Direct the Inventory Committee and MGSO to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively, for local road projects to ensure proper recording and control of the assets.

The MGSO initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the Municipality at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Accountant and MGSO maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and SLCs and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant consider the proposed accounting entries (see Annex F) on advance payment of RPT and SET in order to reflect the correct amount of RPT and SET Income, Due to LGUs and other affected accounts  in the financial statements.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management strictly maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
We also recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is used solely for development projects.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB must strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.
Likewise, we recommended that the LSB strictly comply with the requirements of the said JC in identifying the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenses and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which it was established.
Finally, justification is requested why the non-eligible expenses may not be disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to intensify the review and reconciliation of their records on PPE to come up with reconciled balance of these accounts.
observations.reason: There were supervening events that occur in the course of the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management strictly maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the projects/programs/activities (PPAs) within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
observations.reason: Fortuitous events hindered the maximization of use of funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management maximize the implementation of the identified and prioritized programs to ensure efficient utilization of the fund so that its objectives are fully achieved in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017. 

We also recommended that Management observe the documentary requirements at all times to ensure validity of claims for payment.
observations.reason: Time constraints affected the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Treasurer to update the certified list of real property and special education taxes due and collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card and submit this to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET receivables; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile the balance of the /SET receivables account with the list of taxpayers with paid/unpaid real property tax dues maintained by the Municipal Treasurer to come up with an accurate /SET receivables.
observations.reason:
The limited manpower hindered the updating.



Conflicting schedules of concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that:

Management encourage the Local Disater Risk and Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities,
Management closely monitor the implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRM Fund to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization, 
The Municipal Budget Officer see to it that all charges in the LDRRM Fund are related to four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC 2013-1; and
The Municipal DRRM Officer prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund on time and facilitate monitoring of the accomplishment vis-à-vis the targeted projects for effective and efficient utilization of the fund.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO just assumed office in the first quarter of the year.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted a new 10-year SWM Plan.
observations.reason: Limited budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management update the 2009 Revenue and Tax Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable projects of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Change of legislative members affected the updating.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB identify and prioritize SEF programs based on DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 and ensure that the budget reflects equitable distribution of the funds among the priority activities for an effective utilization of funds and full attainment of its objectives.

We recommended that Management:
	
Require the concerned personnel to secure copies of the Torrens Title or any equivalent document of all land properties registered under the name of the Municipality from the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and ensure proper safekeeping of the newly secured and subsequently acquired Land Titles; 

Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to record the properties over which the Municipality has acquired absolute ownership; and 

Study the possible use of some idle lands with reference to the provisions of RA Nos. 7160 and 7279.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of securing the titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the contractors to undertake immediate completion of the projects otherwise, take appropriate remedies as provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 including the computation and payment of liquidated damages.

The Municipal DRRM Officer prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on time and facilitate monitoring of the accomplishment vis-à-vis the targeted projects for effective and efficient utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management:

Request the concerned officials and employees to liquidate/settle immediately their outstanding cash advances by demanding the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports. Otherwise, withhold any money due them; 

Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash advances; and

Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management: 

Require the BAC, the Secretariat and the TWG to strictly abide by the standards set forth by RA No. 9184, its Revised IRR and other relevant laws on government procurement;  
Require them also to exercise due diligence in the performance of their duties to ensure that all requirements of the procurement law are faithfully adhered to; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 at all times to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit
Also, we recommended that Management observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of approved contracts and their supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) working days from perfection thereof.

b. require th;em also to exercise due diligence in the performance of their duties
to ensure that all requirements of the procurement law are faithfully adhered
to; and |

c. direct the%Alunicipal Accountant to observe the requirements of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 at all times to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit
i
Also, we recommended that Management observe the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of approved contracts and
their supporting documents fo the Auditor within five (5) working days from perfection
thereof.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LSB comply strictly with
the requirements of Section 272 of R4 No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular
Nos. 01 5. 1998, 01-4 s. 2000 and 01-B 5. 2001, as follows:

a. identify the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated
priority expenses and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which
it was established; and

b. maximize the implementation of the identified and prioritized PPAs to ensure
efficient utilization of the furnd so that its objectives are fully achieved.

We reiteraied our prior year's audit recommendation that Management:

a. priovitize, solid waste management  programs and  projects by
providing/allocating enough budget for their full implementation;

b, siricily c&mply with RA No. 9003 on the establishment of solid waste facility;
and |

c. exert continuous effort in educating the people of Juban the importance of
solid waste management.

We recommended that Management ensure that the basic requirements for the
suspension of work and extension of contract time are strictly complied with in
accordance with Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and that proper
documentation is observed af all times to warrant the propriety of the transactions of the
Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s vecommendation that Management require the
GSO and the Accounting Office to maintain Property Cards and PPELCs, respectively,
for proper identification of the PPE of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management conduct disposal of unserviceable properties
in accordance with the process prescribed under COA Circular No. 89-296.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management strictly comply
with DBM and DILG JMC No. 2011-1, as follows:

a. maximize |the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement
the programs, projects and activities within the targeted timeframe to achieve the
social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the
constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all;

b. develop a|concrete plan that will articulate the program, project and activity,
timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and

c. adopt strategies to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of reported
accomplishments to guarantee to the stakeholders the accuracy and reliability
of information that are relevant in decision-making.
